sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.71 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: (J.\,Lauri). A of a finite simple undirected graph$G$of order$n=|V(G)|$is an induced subgraph of order$n-1$. Let$k$be$2,3,4$. For a connected graph$G$with$k$isomorphism types of cards, can$Aut(G)$have more than$k$orbits on the vertex set$V(G)$? If a graph$G$has$k$isomorphism types of cards, then the group$Aut(G)$of automorphisms of$G$has obviously at least$k$orbits on$V(G)$. It is known that if al...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
